meaning: adj.被吓得目瞪口呆的,吓得发懵的
examples: I was dumbstruck by its price of 550 dollars.
I was momentarily dumbstruck by the profound dumbness of this question.
We were dumbstruck. We just couldn't believe our eyes when she appeared.
The visitors were dumbstruck, the impact of what they were seeing, they say, was hard to put into words.
In late May producers were dumbstruck by the sudden appearance of Willie Nelson's braided head on one of the screens.
